body.title: Builder strategy audit — why can't build recurs
body.result: Decision doc written as D-builder-learning-blockade-2026-06-13.md — root cause: Critic exists in devices/critic/ but not wired into DickSimnel ToolLoop; roadmap: simulator → wire Critic → pattern mining
body.intention: I intend that we understand why the 'can't build' failure recurred twice (March Igor, June DickSimnel) and identify the structural pattern.
body.description:
The 'can't build' bug has appeared twice: March in Igor, June in DickSimnel. Both times: builder could execute but couldn't learn (MCP write-blocked). This is not a code bug — it's a design pattern recurring across iterations. ChatGPT pattern analysis shows we have: ReAct, tier-cascade, escalation-as-protocol, skill-compilation. Missing: Critic/Verifier, Event-Sourcing, DAG-workflows, Pattern-Mining.

**Affected files:** Decision document (palace path TBD)
**Design rules:** none apply — this is architecture analysis, not implementation
**Scope boundary:** In: why pattern recurs, what structural fix. Out: implementing the fix (separate tickets)
**Completion criteria:** Decision doc written with: pattern analysis (why recurring), industry patterns we have/miss, critical gap (Critic/Verifier is keystone), integration roadmap (simulator → critic → pattern-mining). Akien reviews and approves as D-builder-learning-blockade-2026-06-13.
